title: Embedded JS in XHTML must escape </script> </style> and ]]> inside user data
description: XHTML CDATA wrappers do NOT prevent HTML parsers from honouring `</script>` or `</style>` sequences inside script/style content. When user/log data is embedded as JS string literals, any literal `</script>` or `</style>` substring (or a `]]>` CDATA terminator) will break the host document. Standard JS \\ and \' escaping is insufficient.
detection: Scan PS sync scripts that build JS-string payloads from external content for the absence of `</script>` and `</style>` neutralisation before write. Flag any Convert-To*JsString helper that does not call [regex]::Replace($x,'(?i)</(script|style)','<\\/$1') or equivalent.
remediation:
Hardened helper:
  $escaped = $sanitized.Replace('\\','\\\\').Replace("'","\\'")
  $escaped = $escaped.Replace(']]>',']]\\u003E')
  $escaped = [regex]::Replace($escaped,'(?i)</(script|style)','<\\/$1')
  $escaped = $escaped.Replace([string][char]0x2028,'\\u2028').Replace([string][char]0x2029,'\\u2029')
Verify post-write that ScriptCloseTags == 1 and JS_PARSE_OK.
examples:
# Bad — log content with literal </script> blows up host XHTML
$js = "var X = '$logContent';"

# Good — neutralise structural sequences first
$safe = (Convert-ToJsString $logContent)
$js = "var X = '$safe';"
